feat: Add advanced features - API integration, auth, forms, and more

## API Integration (Task 5)
- Create Next.js API routes as proxy for JSONPlaceholder API
- Implement CRUD operations for posts and todos
- Integrate SWR for data fetching with optimistic updates
- Add skeleton loaders for loading states
- Implement error handling with toast notifications
- Use styled() API for custom MUI components

## State Management (Task 6)
- Add AuthContext for global authentication state
- Implement user login/logout functionality
- Persist auth state in localStorage
- Theme preference already persisted (ColorModeContext)

## Forms and Validation (Task 7)
- Install react-hook-form, zod, @hookform/resolvers, sonner
- Create login form with email/password validation
- Create signup form with complex validation rules:
  * Name minimum length
  * Email format validation
  * Password strength (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number)
  * Confirm password matching
  * Terms checkbox required
- Display inline validation errors
- Add toast notifications with sonner for success/error messages

## Styling Challenges (Task 8)
- Extensive use of sx prop for MUI style overrides
- Implement styled() API for StyledCard with hover effects
- Responsive layouts throughout (Grid, Stack, flexbox)
- Custom icons using SVG components

## New Features
- Posts management page (/posts) with CRUD operations
- Authentication pages (/auth/login, /auth/signup)
- Protected routes ready (AuthContext)
- User profile menu in header
- Enhanced navigation with auth state
- Skeleton loading components
- Toast notifications system

Refactor: share uuid-to-Ruby conversion between vector and value paths

UUID requires two C helpers because the paths expose different types:
- Vector: duckdb_hugeint (sort-key encoded) -> rbduckdb_uuid_to_ruby
- Value:  duckdb_get_uuid returns duckdb_uhugeint (already decoded) -> rbduckdb_uuid_uhugeint_to_ruby

The conversion logic lives in Ruby:
- _to_uuid_from_vector: XORs the flip bit then formats as UUID string
- _to_uuid_from_uhugeint: formats the decoded uhugeint directly as UUID string

Changes:
- ext/duckdb/conveter.c: Add rbduckdb_uuid_to_ruby and rbduckdb_uuid_uhugeint_to_ruby
- ext/duckdb/converter.h: Declare both new helpers and id__to_uuid_from_uhugeint
- ext/duckdb/result.c: Simplify vector_uuid() to a one-liner
- ext/duckdb/value_impl.c: Add DUCKDB_TYPE_UUID case using duckdb_get_uuid(val)
- lib/duckdb/logical_type.rb: Uncomment uuid: 27
- lib/duckdb/converter.rb: Add _to_uuid_from_uhugeint
- lib/duckdb/scalar_function.rb: Add :uuid to SUPPORTED_TYPES, update doc comments
- test/duckdb_test/expression_test.rb: Add test_fold_returns_string_for_uuid_literal

Part of #1204.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: web-based VNC viewer for remote browser debugging

Expose the browser agent's VNC display through the web app at /vnc,
eliminating the need to expose port 5900 or use a desktop VNC client.

- Add WebSocket-to-TCP proxy (src/browser/vncProxy.ts) bridging browser
  connections to x11vnc on localhost:5900
- Add password auth endpoint (POST /api/vnc/auth) with token-based
  session management (30-min TTL, in-memory store)
- Add VNC viewer page (client/vnc.html) using vendored noVNC v1.5.0
  ESM source with dark theme matching the existing app design
- Add vnc-ws WebSocket path in ws/attach.ts with token validation
- Remove port 5900 from Dockerfile and docker-compose since VNC is
  now tunneled through the app port

Auth flow: password form → validated against VNC_PASSWORD env var →
short-lived token → WebSocket proxy → VNC protocol auth (noVNC handles
the RFB handshake automatically).

commit.message:
fix(ngrx): replace Promise chains with Observable pattern in effects, fix switchMap on mutations, add toast error notifications (#117)

* fix(effects): replace Promise chains with from()+catchError(), fix switchMap on mutations, add toast notifications

- Convert all Promise .then().catch() chains in board, board-list, board-template,
  activity, and user-profile effects to the safe from()+pipe(map(),catchError())
  Observable pattern. This prevents edge-case errors from escaping the inner
  observable and killing the actions$ stream (which silently dropped the current
  action on NgRx re-subscription).
- Fix task.effects.ts: change switchMap -> mergeMap for addTask$, updateTask$,
  deleteTask$ so rapid/programmatic double-dispatches no longer cancel in-flight
  form saves.
- Inject ToastrService into all effects files and show error toasts in every
  failure path (API exceptions via catchError and logical failures via map when
  the API returns null/false).

commit.message:
Refactor: share timestamp_tz-to-Ruby conversion between vector and value paths

Extract rbduckdb_timestamp_tz_to_ruby(duckdb_timestamp) into conveter.c
following the same approach as previous timestamp converters (PR #1213).

TIMESTAMP_TZ is stored in vectors as UTC microseconds accessed through
duckdb_time_tz.bits (uint64_t); duckdb_get_timestamp_tz() returns the
same value as duckdb_timestamp.micros (int64_t). The shared helper takes
duckdb_timestamp; the vector side casts bits via (int64_t).

- vector_timestamp_tz() in result.c becomes a one-liner delegating to
  the shared helper
- rbduckdb_duckdb_value_to_ruby() in value_impl.c gains a new
  DUCKDB_TYPE_TIMESTAMP_TZ case via duckdb_get_timestamp_tz(val)
- Add :timestamp_tz to ScalarFunction::SUPPORTED_TYPES
- Add test_fold_returns_time_for_timestamp_tz_literal in expression_test.rb
  (uses return_type: :bigint since DuckDB does not support TIMESTAMPTZ as a
  scalar function return type; asserts utc_offset == 0 as TIMESTAMPTZ
  always normalises to UTC)

Part of #1204.

Test: assert utc_offset in test_fold_returns_time_for_time_tz_literal

The whole point of TIMETZ is to carry a timezone offset. Without asserting
utc_offset a regression that drops or corrupts the offset would go undetected.

+05:30 = 5*3600 + 30*60 = 19_800 seconds.

Initial open-core extraction from t1pal-mobile-workspace

9 AGPL-3.0 packages extracted as standalone SPM package 'T1PalOpenCore':
- T1PalCore: Foundation types, persistence (GRDB), glucose model
- T1PalCompatKit: Platform detection, OS abstraction
- BLEKit: CoreBluetooth abstraction layer
- CryptoValidation: EC crypto for Dexcom G7 J-PAKE
- T1PalAlgorithm: Algorithm adapters (oref0, Loop, OpenAPS)
- NightscoutKit: Nightscout REST API client
- CGMKit: Dexcom G6/G7, Libre 2/3 protocol implementations
- CGMKitShare: Dexcom Share + LibreLinkUp cloud clients
- PumpKit: Omnipod DASH/Eros, Tandem X2 protocols
- CLinuxOpenSSL: System library shim for Linux builds

753 Swift source files, 250 JSON fixtures. Builds standalone on Linux.
Designed to be consumed via SPM by both the monorepo workspace and
the rag-nightscout-ecosystem-alignment research repo.

commit.message:
Phase 3: Integration layer + MCP market data exposure

Add orchestrator, MCP server, and new trading endpoints:

1. backend/orchestrator.py
   - Central control flow connecting Modules 1, 2, 3
   - Monitors signal changes (detects BUY/SELL switches)
   - Validates trading rules before execution (risk checks)
   - Executes trades when conditions met (min 60% confidence)
   - Thread-safe monitoring of positions and signals
   - Configurable signal check interval (30s) and position update (10s)
   - User-controllable auto-trading toggle

2. backend/mcp_server.py (Market Context Protocol)
   - Exposes market data as MCP resources (market:// URIs)
   - MCP tools: query_market_data, query_signals, query_positions, check_trading_rules
   - Enables external AI agents to query market state
   - Read-only access to prevent accidental data corruption
   - Resources: latest-prices, signals, positions, risk-status, account

3. backend/server.py - New endpoints:
   - GET /api/positions - open positions with P&L
   - GET /api/portfolio - account summary (equity, cash, daily loss)
   - GET /api/tax-log - trade history (JSON or CSV download)
   - POST /api/trade - manual trade execution
   - POST /api/toggle-auto-trade - user auto-trading control
   - GET /api/orchestrator-status - orchestrator state and signals
   - Orchestrator starts automatically as daemon thread

4. requirements.txt
   - Added mcp>=0.1.0 for Model Context Protocol support

Features:
✅ Signal-based trading with confidence filtering
✅ Automated position monitoring
✅ MCP market data exposure for AI integration
✅ User-controlled auto-trading toggle
✅ Full audit trail for compliance
✅ Real-time orchestration feedback

Architecture:
Module 1 (data) → Module 2 (signals) → Orchestrator → Module 3 (trading)
                                            ↓
                                    MCP Server (external AI access)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
